hypothesis: A dropout schedule driven by parameter-to-data and compute-to-data pressure will reduce final validation cross-entropy relative to matched static-dropout baselines across the measured streaming language-model regimes.
claimBoundary: The evidence covers three datasets, two token-prefix budgets, small decoder-only transformers, and three to five paired seeds; it does not establish universal coefficients, frontier-scale transfer, or superiority over every possible adaptive regularizer.

baselines: best matched static-dropout condition selected within each regime
per-seed static counterpart at the same dataset, architecture, and prefix budget
controls: earliest-stage comparison where the decay schedule is often worse
dataset-specific coefficient fits with leave-model and leave-prefix diagnostics
